title: DSDS Imports Document Block
description: How to install and import a component in code, across platforms — also reused on guides for setup steps. One entry per platform (React, Vue, Web Components, etc.), each with the package name and the import code.

importEntry.description: One platform's import instructions — which platform, and the code to import and use the component.

platform.description: The platform this applies to (e.g., 'react', 'vue', 'ios'). Use the same identifiers as the `api` block. Left out, it's assumed to be the only/default platform.

package.description: The package to install (e.g., '@acme/ui'), exactly as it appears in the package manager.

code.description: The import code. SHOULD be copy-paste ready. Use newlines for multi-line snippets.

language.description: The language or format of the code (e.g., 'tsx', 'js', 'vue', 'swift', 'kotlin'). Omit when obvious from the platform.

description.description: Extra context — when to use this path over another, version requirements, or setup steps.

since.description: The version in which this import path became available.

imports.description: How to import a component across platforms, one entry per platform. List the main/recommended platform first.

kind.description: Identifies this block as an imports spec.

items.description: The per-platform import entries, primary platform first.
